Introduction.name: Juyeon Won
Introduction.body: I solely own PrivKeeper, a unified password-management solution for CCTV/surveillance environments, leading root-cause analysis across the Java–JNI–C++ boundary, the national security-function verification, and a full legacy-stack modernization.

About.title: About Me
About.paragraph-1: I'm a backend & security engineer whose strength is tracing failures that span Java, JNI, native C++, external SDKs, and the network all the way down to the real root cause.
About.paragraph-2: I've been the sole owner of PrivKeeper (Java web + C++/JNI), a unified password-management solution for CCTV/surveillance environments — across its full lifecycle, from feature development to the national security-function verification, full legacy-stack modernization, and field response.
About.paragraph-3: I work from the principle that 'even if the log says success, you must cross-verify the real state' — digging into problems based on actual behavior and evidence, not the surface result.
About.paragraph-4: I lead security work (finding and fixing vulnerabilities, applied cryptography) and EOL-library modernization end to end — risk analysis → phased rollout → verification — and I integrate heterogeneous devices with wildly different vendors and protocols into an extensible structure.
About.paragraph-5: When something is inconvenient or repetitive, I don't let it slide — I build tools myself (OnviProbe, utilApp) to improve it, and I apply new tech (LLM/RAG) directly to real work and projects.

privkeeperp.title: PrivKeeper — Unified Password-Management Solution (main work)
privkeeperp.description: I solely own PrivKeeper, a unified password-management solution for CCTV/surveillance environments (Java web + C++/JNI). I built automatic password changes across heterogeneous devices and led the national security-function verification and a full legacy-stack modernization (JDK 25, Spring 7). Root-cause analysis across the Java–JNI–C++ boundary is my strength.

onviprobe.title: OnviProbe — ONVIF Camera Diagnostic Tool
onviprobe.description: A Go tool I built on my own initiative to cut down on recurring field issues — it predicts whether PrivKeeper can change a given camera's password without touching the camera. It reproduces the product's ONVIF decision logic, adds an independent ONVIF-standard verdict, and categorizes failure causes into codes.

utilapp.title: utilApp — Broadcast Encryption (CAS) TS/EMM Analyzer
utilapp.description: A tool I built that's now in real internal use — a Qt/C++ diagnostic app that lets you check, on site, whether a broadcast (CAS) stream is encrypted and whether EMM is being received. Another case of 'if it's inconvenient, I build it.'

aiauto.title: PrivKeeper Incident-Response Automation (AI · LLM)
aiauto.description: An internal automation tool I built while learning to apply LLMs — it auto-classifies customer inquiries/incidents with Gemini·GPT and drafts responses and similar-case references.

cctv.title: CCTV Anomaly-Response RAG Solution (Government R&D · VLM Project)
cctv.description: Owning the RAG/LLM part of a government R&D VLM project. Building a RAG knowledge base from guideline documents and designing an auto-generation pipeline (ChromaDB + embeddings + local LLM) for situational action recommendations and reports; currently preparing for performance evaluation.

more.text: Most of the projects above are internal/security products, so the source is private.

Contact.title: Contact Me
Contact.intro: If you'd like to collaborate on a product, or reach out about a role,
Contact.cta: you can contact me at wjy6454@naver.com or via the links below.

training.name: C++ Image-Processing Developer Program — Korea Software Industry Association
notes: Built a medical-imaging (dental X-ray) pre/post-processing viewer — grand prize at the final project showcase.
Solid C++ foundations: CUDA parallel programming, Qt GUI, SQLite, and TCP/IP sockets.

univ.name: B.S. in Biomedical Engineering — Konyang University
notes: Participated in an undergraduate research group on U-Net-based cell detection/counting (intro to deep learning & medical imaging).

bullets: Sole owner of PrivKeeper, a unified password-management solution for CCTV/surveillance environments (Java web + C++/JNI native), across its full lifecycle — feature development, the national security-function verification, library modernization, and field response (~110 commits over 2.5 years in the web repo).

Expanded managed devices from cameras → Linux/Windows servers → network switches, designing switches so a new device is supported just by adding a JSON template from the console output — no code changes.

Handled the national security-function verification — adapting product features to the national security guidelines (integrity verification, session/account security, audit logs) and reconciling the submitted deliverables with the code.

Modernized the legacy stack end to end — from libcurl/OpenSSL/Log4j/PostgreSQL/JDBC to a JDK 25 + Tomcat 11 + Spring/Security 7 + Jakarta migration, via risk analysis → phased rollout → traffic-capture verification.
Early on, handled GS certification for a video-anonymization product (PrivKeeper M, C#); currently owning the RAG/LLM part of a government R&D VLM project.
